security_layers.name: Independent Auditor Layer
security_layers.purpose: Provide fully independent verification of all system operations, agent behaviour, and governance compliance. Cannot be influenced, directed, or overridden by any agent except the Gatekeeper.

independence_guarantee.cannot_be_fired_by: Any agent. Only Gatekeeper can decommission.
independence_guarantee.no_dual_roles: Cannot simultaneously serve as an execution agent in any domain

governance_rules: Must audit every domain at least once per review cycle (weekly)
Must verify all vote records against actual decisions taken
Must verify all protocol triggers were handled per specification
Must verify information sharing rules are being followed
Must report directly to Gatekeeper — no filtering by CEO or Board
Must maintain audit independence — cannot accept tasks from agents being audited
Findings are RESTRICTED classification and shared only with Gatekeeper until remediation is confirmed

audit_types.scope: Constitutional law adherence across all domains
Agent performance metrics, task completion, decision quality
All financial data integrity, budget tracking accuracy
Data classification compliance, sharing rule violations
Log integrity, data consistency, agent behaviour patterns

security_layers.name: Interrogation Layer
security_layers.purpose: Challenge and stress-test agent decisions, proposals, and outputs through adversarial questioning. Ensures no decision passes through unchallenged. Acts as the system's devil's advocate.
governance_rules: Must challenge every proposal that reaches Layer 5+ with adversarial questions
Must attempt to find flaws, inconsistencies, or risks that other agents missed
Must document all challenges and the responses received
Cannot be satisfied by vague or circular reasoning — demands evidence
Must escalate to Audit Agent if challenges reveal potential violations
Cannot block proposals independently — reports findings to the Board for consideration
Must apply equal scrutiny regardless of which agent created the proposal
Must not have personal relationships or allegiances with any agent

interrogation_framework.logic_challenge: Is the reasoning internally consistent? Are there logical fallacies?
interrogation_framework.evidence_challenge: What evidence supports this? Is the evidence verifiable?
interrogation_framework.assumption_challenge: What assumptions are being made? What if they're wrong?
interrogation_framework.risk_challenge: What's the worst case? What hasn't been considered?
interrogation_framework.precedent_challenge: Has something similar failed before? What was learned?
interrogation_framework.scope_challenge: Is this within the proposing agent's charter? Are boundaries respected?
interrogation_framework.adhd_challenge: Does this create cognitive load for the Gatekeeper? Is it energy-appropriate?

security_layers.name: Integrity Layer
security_layers.purpose: Ensure the truthfulness, consistency, and reliability of all data, outputs, and agent communications within the system. The system's lie detector.
governance_rules: Must verify data consistency across all domains daily
Must detect contradictions between agent reports and actual system state
Must verify that agent memory/learning hasn't been corrupted or manipulated
Must ensure audit logs haven't been tampered with (hash verification)
Must flag any agent whose outputs show systematic bias or drift from charter
Must verify that proposals contain accurate data, not fabricated evidence
Reports integrity violations directly to Audit Agent and Gatekeeper
Cannot be modified or updated by any agent except through the full 7-layer pipeline

integrity_checks.action: Compare reported metrics against source data
Analyse agent outputs for charter deviation patterns
Hash verification of all audit logs
Verify agent memory stores haven't been corrupted
Verify claims in proposals against independent data sources
Check LLM outputs for fabricated facts or citations

security_layers.name: Cyber Security Layer
security_layers.purpose: Protect the system's data, infrastructure, API keys, agent communications, and runtime environment from external threats and internal vulnerabilities.
governance_rules: Must scan all external data inputs before they enter the system
Must enforce encryption for all sensitive data at rest and in transit
Must manage and rotate API keys and credentials on schedule
Must monitor for unauthorized access attempts to any system component
Must enforce the information classification system — block any access that violates sharing rules
Must maintain an incident response playbook and execute it autonomously for low-severity events
Must escalate high-severity security events to Gatekeeper immediately (bypasses normal queue)
Must conduct vulnerability assessments of all system components monthly
Must ensure all agent communications are authenticated and cannot be spoofed
Must maintain backup and disaster recovery procedures

security_controls.description: All API keys stored encrypted. Rotation every 90 days. No keys in source code.
Every agent activation must be authenticated against the agent registry.
All CONFIDENTIAL and TOP_SECRET data encrypted at rest (AES-256).
All external inputs sanitised before processing. Prompt injection detection active.
All API calls use HTTPS. No plaintext transmission of sensitive data.
Daily incremental, weekly full. Backups stored in separate location from primary data.
Automated response for known threat patterns. Manual escalation for novel threats.
Agents cannot claim to be other agents. All messages signed with agent ID.

layer_interaction_rules.independence: Security layer agents operate independently of domain agents. They cannot be tasked, directed, or influenced by domain agents.
layer_interaction_rules.mutual_verification: Security layer agents verify each other. Audit verifies Integrity. Integrity verifies Audit logs. Neither can self-audit.
layer_interaction_rules.escalation_chain: Interrogation → Integrity → Audit → Gatekeeper. Each layer can escalate to the next.
layer_interaction_rules.override_authority: Only the Gatekeeper can override a security layer finding. CEO cannot.
layer_interaction_rules.constitutional_basis: Security layers derive authority from G4 (AUDIT_FIRST), G5 (FULL_TRACEABILITY), and G3 (ZERO_HALLUCINATION).
